title: AMD and Cerebras partner on AI inference with Helios system
excerpt: AMD is betting the future of AI won't rely on a single chip, unveiling a Cerebras partnership and new Helios system to challenge Nvidia.
content_html:
AMD takes a shot at Nvidia by betting on AI's next big shift

By Geoff Weiss

You're currently following this author! Want to unfollow? Unsubscribe via the link in your email.

AMD is making a bet about the future of AI: one chip shouldn't rule them all.

CEO Lisa Su announced Thursday that AMD is teaming up with chip startup Cerebras on a new approach to AI inference, which is the process of generating responses from AI models.

Increasingly, chipmakers are pursuing "disaggregated inference," which splits workloads across different types of hardware. AMD's partnership with Cerebras shows the company is betting big on this approach.

Traditionally, the same hardware handled both processing a prompt and generating an answer. AMD argues those are fundamentally different jobs. Helios, its latest server system, is designed to process huge volumes of requests, whereas Cerebras' giant, wafer-sized chip specializes in generating near-instantaneous responses.

The partnership will bring Helios into Cerebras' data centers later this year.

Demand for chips from companies like AMD, Nvidia, and Broadcom has skyrocketed in the AI boom. Nvidia dominates chip design for AI training, and the competition has intensified as AI companies shift focus from training models to putting them to work.

The AMD and Cerebras pact aligns with a broader shift that analysts say is already underway, with UBS writing in June that the limitations of current architectures "are driving a shift toward disaggregated inference."

UBS wrote that Nvidia — through its integration of AI hardware startup Groq — and Amazon Web Services are also pursuing similar setups to improve efficiency and lower costs. That said, UBS wrote that disaggregated inference presents new challenges around "orchestration" — or getting different chips to work together seamlessly.

At Advancing AI, AMD unveiled Helios, its latest server system that bundles several types of AI chips, which is its answer to Nvidia's Vera Rubin NVL72 rack. AI labs and cloud giants using AMD's infrastructure include OpenAI, Meta, Microsoft, Oracle, and Anthropic, with which AMD announced a multibillion-dollar infrastructure partnership on Wednesday.

AMD also used the event to take direct aim at Nvidia, claiming that Helios delivers up to 30% more inference tokens per dollar than Nvidia's Vera Rubin NVL72 rack.

"Every Helios can deliver more performance for the largest models, more capacity for longer context, and the bandwidth to scale across thousands of racks," Su said Thursday at AMD's Advancing AI event.

Have a tip? Contact this reporter via email at gweiss@businessinsider.com or Signal at @geoffweiss.25. Use a personal email address, a nonwork WiFi network, and a nonwork device; here's our guide to sharing information securely.

content_html_zh:
AMD 押注人工智能的下一个重大转变，向英伟达发起攻击

作者：Geoff Weiss 您目前正在关注该作者！ 想要取消关注吗？ 通过电子邮件中的链接取消订阅。

AMD 正在对人工智能的未来进行赌注：一款芯片不应该统治一切。

首席执行官 Lisa Su 周四宣布，AMD 正在与芯片初创公司 Cerebras 合作开发一种新的人工智能推理方法，即从人工智能模型生成响应的过程。

芯片制造商越来越多地追求“分类推理”，将工作负载分配到不同类型的硬件上。 AMD 与 Cerebras 的合作表明该公司对这种方法下了很大的赌注。

传统上，相同的硬件负责处理提示和生成答案。 AMD 认为这些是根本不同的工作。 Helios 是其最新的服务器系统，旨在处理大量请求，而 Cerebras 的巨型晶圆大小芯片专门用于生成近乎即时的响应。

此次合作将于今年晚些时候将 Helios 引入 Cerebras 的数据中心。

在人工智能热潮中，AMD、英伟达和博通等公司对芯片的需求猛增。 英伟达在人工智能训练芯片设计领域占据主导地位，随着人工智能公司将重点从训练模型转向将模型投入实际应用，竞争也愈演愈烈。

AMD 和 Cerebras 的协议与分析师称已经在进行的更广泛的转变相一致，瑞银 (UBS) 在 6 月份撰文称，当前架构的局限性“正在推动向分类推理的转变”。

瑞银写道，英伟达（通过与人工智能硬件初创公司 Groq 的整合）和亚马逊网络服务也在寻求类似的设置，以提高效率并降低成本。 尽管如此，瑞银写道，分类推理给“编排”带来了新的挑战，即让不同的芯片无缝地协同工作。

在 Advancing AI 大会上，AMD 推出了 Helios，这是其最新的服务器系统，捆绑了多种类型的 AI 芯片，这是对 Nvidia 的 Vera Rubin NVL72 机架的回应。 使用 AMD 基础设施的人工智能实验室和云巨头包括 OpenAI、Meta、微软、甲骨文和 Anthropic，AMD 于周三宣布与这些公司建立价值数十亿美元的基础设施合作伙伴关系。

AMD 还利用这次活动直接瞄准了 Nvidia，声称 Helios 每美元提供的推理代币比 Nvidia 的 Vera Rubin NVL72 机架多出 30%。

“每个 Helios 都可以为最大的模型提供更高的性能，为更长的环境提供更大的容量，以及在数千个机架上扩展的带宽，”Su 周四在 AMD 的推进人工智能活动上表示。

有小费吗？ 通过电子邮件 gweiss@businessinsider.com 或 Signal @geoffweiss.25 联系本记者。 使用个人电子邮件地址、非工作 WiFi 网络和非工作设备；这是我们安全共享信息的指南。

Geoff Weiss 是 Business Insider 技术团队的高级记者，撰写有关 AI 初创公司和 Y Combinator、AI 与媒体行业的交叉点，以及顶级 AI 实验室和芯片公司的工作场所动态的文章。 此前，Geoff 在媒体台工作，报道 YouTube 和 Netflix，以及好莱坞与创作者经济的交叉点等主题。 他在 Netflix 视频播客雄心方面的工作和《野兽先生》对好莱坞的教训分别在 2025 年洛杉矶新闻俱乐部奖中获得二等奖和一等奖。 在加入 Business Insider 之前，Geoff 是 Tubefilter 的高级编辑和 Entrepreneur 的特约撰稿人。 他毕业于纽约大学，获得英国文学学位。可以通过 gweiss@businessinsider.com、Signal @geoffweiss.25 和 LinkedIn 联系他。 有小费吗？ 使用个人电子邮件地址和非工作设备；这是我们安全共享信息的指南。 精选故事：英伟达季度表现不佳，首席执行官黄仁勋在一份泄露的全体会议上表示，“市场对此并不欣赏”。英伟达将为特朗普的新签证费用买单。 首席执行官黄仁勋 (Jensen Huang) 向员工表示如下。 海量的人工智能薪资和 RTO 正在推动旧金山的房地产繁荣：“金钱将会倾盆而下”人工智能人才之战正在初创公司中掀起。 以下是他们与大型科技公司竞争的方式。
